summary.intent: Address three PR review comments on PR #784 in the moltis repo: deduplicate tail_lines into a shared module, fix a two-lock pattern with optimistic insert + rollback, and add tests for the provisioning guard.
summary.outcome: All three review comments were resolved: tail_lines moved to types.rs, the two-lock pattern fixed in docker.rs, provisioning guard tests added (7 new tests, 36 total passing), formatting and clippy clean, committed and pushed.

repo: Shared helpers for the sandbox module live in crates/tools/src/sandbox/types.rs (e.g., truncate_output_for_display), making it the right home for utility functions like tail_lines.
Sandbox tests are organized under crates/tools/src/sandbox/tests/ with core.rs as the primary test file.
The moltis repo is located at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/jeweled-wake/ and uses worktrees for development.
DockerSandbox uses a two-lock provisioning pattern that required an optimistic insert + rollback approach.

code.finding: The provisioned field on DockerSandbox was private and needed to be changed to pub(crate) to allow test access from the tests module.
tail_lines function was consolidated here from duplicate implementations in docker.rs and host.rs.

workflow: When struct fields are private and need test visibility in Rust, pub(crate) is the minimal visibility change rather than fully public.
Running fmt and clippy after logic changes before committing catches style issues early.
Fetching PR review comments via Bash tooling allows systematic enumeration of all comments before starting fixes.

friction: The provisioned field being private caused a compile error in tests, requiring a visibility change to pub(crate) — discovered only at test-compile time.
tail_lines was duplicated in both docker.rs and host.rs, requiring edits to three files (types.rs, docker.rs, host.rs) to consolidate it.
